name: D+Q bone RCT 20-week BMD synthesis compatibility
question: Can the 20-week BMD result from the D+Q postmenopausal bone RCT be pooled for a meta-analysis estimate?

compatibility_basis.population: Postmenopausal women in the D+Q and untreated-control arms of one phase 2 randomized trial.
compatibility_basis.endpoint_family: DXA bone mineral density percent change by lumbar spine, femoral neck, and radius site.
compatibility_basis.effect_metric: Site-specific between-group BMD percent-change effect with uncertainty.
compatibility_basis.variance_model: No pooling model selected because the registry record is descriptive, site-combined, and lacks pairwise site-specific effects with uncertainty.

pooling_rationale: Pooling is blocked by result count and descriptive-only extraction. The BMD record contains site-stratified group medians and full ranges, but no posted pairwise D+Q versus control analysis for an overall BMD endpoint and no site-specific effect values or uncertainty estimates. Lumbar spine, femoral neck, and radius values should not be collapsed into one pooled effect without site-specific outcome definitions.
non_pooling_reason: Do not pool BMD until site-specific outcomes or a pre-specified composite BMD metric are extracted with effect values, uncertainty, comparison, sample size, and group-value fields from at least two compatible results.

missing_effect_fields_by_result.note: The result is descriptive-only: it has site-stratified group summaries but no pairwise overall or site-specific D+Q effect value with variance-compatible uncertainty.

self_healing_actions: Keep BMD pooling blocked until site-specific effect records are extracted or a defensible composite BMD outcome is defined.
Route BMD extraction to extraction_refresh if full text or supplements provide site-level pairwise estimates with uncertainty.

provenance.note: Posted BMD group values by lumbar spine, femoral neck, and radius classes.

provenance.locator: PubMed abstract exploratory BMD subgroup sentence
provenance.note: Abstract mentions an exploratory radius BMD subgroup signal, which is not the same as the overall registry-posted BMD summary.

name: D+Q bone RCT 20-week CTx synthesis compatibility
question: Can the 20-week CTx result from the D+Q postmenopausal bone RCT be pooled for a meta-analysis estimate?

compatibility_basis.population: Postmenopausal women in the D+Q and untreated-control arms of one phase 2 randomized trial.
compatibility_basis.effect_metric: Between-group CTx percent-change effect with uncertainty.
compatibility_basis.variance_model: No pooling model selected because only one result is available and the effect record is p-value-only.

pooling_rationale: Pooling is blocked by result count and extraction completeness. This endpoint-specific group has one CTx result from one trial, while the pooling requirement is at least two compatible results. The registry record contains arm medians, full ranges, and a Wilcoxon p-value, but no extractable between-group effect value or CI, standard error, or variance.
non_pooling_reason: Do not pool this primary CTx endpoint until at least one additional compatible CTx result is available and every included result has an effect value, uncertainty estimate, comparison, sample size, and required group-value fields.

missing_effect_fields_by_result.note: The result is a p-value-only overall CTx comparison with group medians and full ranges; full range is not a variance estimate, and only one endpoint result is available.

self_healing_actions: Keep this endpoint group in pooling_blocked state until effect uncertainty and at least one additional compatible CTx result are available.
Route p-value-only CTx extraction to extraction_refresh if full text or supplements provide a between-group estimate with uncertainty.

provenance.note: Posted primary CTx outcome group values and D+Q versus untreated-control p-value.
Abstract confirms no significant overall CTx difference between groups.

name: D+Q bone high-p16 T3 exploratory subgroup synthesis compatibility
question: Can exploratory high-p16 T3 subgroup effects from the D+Q postmenopausal bone RCT be pooled for a meta-analysis estimate?

compatibility_basis.population: Postmenopausal women in the highest tertile of baseline T cell p16 mRNA levels within one phase 2 randomized trial.
compatibility_basis.comparator: Untreated control within the high-p16 T3 subgroup.
compatibility_basis.endpoint_family: Exploratory subgroup P1NP, CTx, and distal radius BMD percent-change endpoints.
compatibility_basis.time_horizon: Baseline to 2 weeks, 4 weeks, or approximately 20 weeks depending on endpoint.
compatibility_basis.effect_metric: Relative percent difference versus untreated control within the exploratory T3 subgroup.
compatibility_basis.variance_model: No variance model selected because records come from one exploratory subgroup analysis, mix marker identities and time horizons, and lack CI, standard error, or variance.

pooling_rationale: These records are useful for hypothesis tracking and subgroup-aware evidence maps, but they should not be pooled as one meta-analytic endpoint. They come from one exploratory post hoc subgroup in a single trial, mix P1NP, CTx, and radius BMD endpoints, use different time horizons, and report p-values without CI, standard error, or variance.
non_pooling_reason: Keep high-p16 T3 subgroup effects separate from overall randomized endpoints and from each other unless future compatible records share marker identity, time horizon, subgroup definition, effect metric, and uncertainty.

missing_effect_fields_by_result.note: The result reports effect value and p-value, but no CI, standard error, or variance.
The result reports effect value and p-value, but no CI, standard error, or variance.
The result reports effect value and p-value, but no CI, standard error, or variance.
The result reports effect value and p-value, but no CI, standard error, or variance.
The result reports effect value and p-value, but no CI, standard error, or variance.

self_healing_actions: Keep high-p16 T3 subgroup records separated from overall randomized endpoint records.
Only create a pooled subgroup synthesis when future records share subgroup definition, marker identity, time horizon, and uncertainty fields.
Route supplement/table extraction to a scoped extraction agent if variance or CI fields become available from lawful source snapshots.

provenance.note: Exploratory/post hoc analysis section reports high-p16 T3 subgroup effects and subgroup sample sizes for P1NP, CTx, and BMD endpoints.

name: D+Q bone RCT 20-week P1NP synthesis compatibility
question: Can the 20-week P1NP result from the D+Q postmenopausal bone RCT be pooled for a meta-analysis estimate?

compatibility_basis.population: Postmenopausal women in the D+Q and untreated-control arms of one phase 2 randomized trial.
compatibility_basis.effect_metric: Between-group P1NP percent-change effect with uncertainty.
compatibility_basis.variance_model: No pooling model selected because only one 20-week P1NP result is available and the effect record is p-value-only.

pooling_rationale: Pooling is blocked by result count and missing effect extraction. This endpoint-specific group has one 20-week P1NP result from one trial, and the posted record provides arm medians, full ranges, and a p-value rather than a between-group effect value with uncertainty.
non_pooling_reason: Do not pool the 20-week P1NP endpoint until at least two compatible 20-week P1NP results are available and all included results carry effect value, uncertainty, comparison, sample-size, and group-value fields.

missing_effect_fields_by_result.note: The 20-week P1NP result is p-value-only with group medians and full ranges; it lacks a between-group effect value and variance-compatible uncertainty.

self_healing_actions: Keep this endpoint group separate from short-term P1NP signals because the 20-week time horizon and direction differ.
Route p-value-only 20-week P1NP extraction to extraction_refresh if source tables provide an effect estimate with uncertainty.

provenance.note: Posted 20-week P1NP group values and D+Q versus untreated-control p-value.

provenance.locator: PubMed abstract P1NP secondary endpoint sentence
provenance.note: Abstract reports P1NP was not different from untreated control at 20 weeks.

name: D+Q bone RCT short-term P1NP synthesis compatibility
question: Can the short-term 2-week and 4-week P1NP results from the D+Q postmenopausal bone RCT be pooled for a meta-analysis estimate?

compatibility_basis.population: Postmenopausal women in the D+Q and untreated-control arms of one phase 2 randomized trial.
compatibility_basis.time_horizon: Baseline to 2 weeks and baseline to 4 weeks; not a single compatible time horizon.
compatibility_basis.effect_metric: Percentage-point difference relative to control with uncertainty.
compatibility_basis.variance_model: No pooling model selected because the two short-term timepoints are repeated measures from one trial and lack uncertainty estimates.

pooling_rationale: Pooling is blocked despite two result records because the records are different time horizons from the same trial, not independent compatible studies. Both encode a +16 percentage-point short-term P1NP signal, but neither result has a CI, standard error, or variance. A formal synthesis would need either a pre-specified single time horizon or a longitudinal/repeated-measures model plus uncertainty.
non_pooling_reason: Do not compute a pooled short-term P1NP estimate across the 2-week and 4-week records unless the time horizon is made compatible and each result has an uncertainty estimate; treat these as related short-term signals in narrative synthesis.

missing_effect_fields_by_result.note: The 2-week result has an effect value but no CI, standard error, or variance, and it should not be pooled directly with the 4-week timepoint as the same horizon.
The 4-week result has an effect value but no CI, standard error, or variance, and it should not be pooled directly with the 2-week timepoint as the same horizon.

self_healing_actions: Keep 2-week and 4-week P1NP as non-pooled related signals unless a longitudinal synthesis plan is added.
Route P1NP extraction to extraction_refresh if full text or supplements provide uncertainty estimates for either short-term timepoint.

provenance.note: Posted 2-week and 4-week P1NP group values and D+Q versus untreated-control p-values.

provenance.locator: PubMed abstract P1NP secondary endpoint sentence
provenance.note: Abstract reports +16 percent relative to control at 2 and 4 weeks with nominal p-values.

name: D+Q bone RCT registry-extracted synthesis compatibility
question: Can the D+Q postmenopausal bone RCT registry-extracted endpoint results be pooled for a meta-analysis estimate?

compatibility_basis.population: Postmenopausal women in the D+Q and untreated-control arms of one phase 2 randomized trial.
compatibility_basis.endpoint_family: Bone turnover, bone density, SASP, and aggregate safety endpoints.
compatibility_basis.time_horizon: Baseline to 2, 4, or 20 weeks depending on endpoint.
compatibility_basis.effect_metric: Percent change, descriptive group summaries, or p-value-only effects depending on endpoint.
compatibility_basis.variance_model: No pooling model selected because effect uncertainty fields are absent or incompatible.

pooling_rationale: The grouped results are useful for an evidence map, but they mix primary, secondary, biomarker, BMD, SASP, and safety endpoints across different time horizons. Several records only provide p-values or descriptive summaries, and the registry full-range dispersion is not a variance estimate suitable for pooling.
non_pooling_reason: Do not compute a pooled estimate until endpoint-specific compatibility groups contain at least two comparable studies and each included result has an effect value plus CI, standard error, or variance.

missing_effect_fields_by_result.note: The registry record has group medians and a p-value, but no extractable effect value or variance estimate.
The effect value is available, but no CI, standard error, or variance is encoded.
The effect value is available, but no CI, standard error, or variance is encoded.
The registry record has group medians and a p-value, but no extractable effect value or variance estimate.
The BMD record is descriptive and combines multiple DXA sites rather than one compatible endpoint metric.
The registry record has group medians and a p-value, but no extractable effect value or variance estimate.
Aggregate adverse-event counts should be represented in a safety-specific compatibility group before any effect estimate is attempted.

self_healing_actions: Keep this synthesis group in pooling_blocked state until uncertainty fields are extracted or a compatible endpoint-specific group is created.
Route missing effect fields to extraction_refresh when PubMed full text, supplements, or registry analysis tables can support them.
Regenerate consumer exports after any referenced outcome, result, or synthesis-group record changes.

name: D+Q bone RCT 20-week safety synthesis compatibility
question: Can the 20-week aggregate and headache preferred-term adverse-event results from the D+Q postmenopausal bone RCT be pooled for a safety meta-analysis estimate?

compatibility_basis.population: Postmenopausal women in the D+Q and untreated-control arms of one phase 2 randomized trial.
compatibility_basis.endpoint_family: Adverse events, headache preferred-term adverse event, serious adverse events, withdrawals, and deaths through approximately 20 weeks.
compatibility_basis.effect_metric: Event-specific risk ratio or risk difference with preferred-term counts and explicit zero/not-reported handling.
compatibility_basis.variance_model: No safety pooling model selected because the available records remain descriptive, single-trial, and lack comparative effect estimates with uncertainty.

pooling_rationale: Pooling remains blocked after adding the headache-specific result. The new result preserves the D+Q headache count from Extended Data Table 1 and the blank control-cell ambiguity, but it does not provide a defensible D+Q versus control risk ratio, risk difference, CI, standard error, or variance. The current result schema also cannot directly encode adverse_event.preferred_term or adverse_event.event_specific_counts as structured fields.
non_pooling_reason: Do not pool safety until adverse events are represented with structured preferred terms, event-specific arm counts with explicit zero/not-reported semantics, a valid comparison, and effect uncertainty from at least two compatible safety results.

missing_effect_fields_by_result.note: The result is an aggregate safety_event record, not preferred-term event-specific extraction; it lacks comparative effect value, uncertainty, and explicit event-specific preferred-term counts.

The publication table extraction provides descriptive preferred-term counts for reported terms, but it lacks structured adverse-event term fields, comparative effect value, uncertainty, and explicit zero/not-reported semantics for blank cells.

The term-specific result records the headache row using valid result fields and the D+Q 16/30 group value, but the result schema has no structured adverse_event object and the control cell remains blank/ambiguous rather than an explicit event count.

self_healing_actions: Keep safety pooling blocked until adverse events are split into term-specific result records with explicit zero/not-reported semantics.
Extend the result schema before treating adverse_event.preferred_term or adverse_event.event_specific_counts as directly structured extraction fields.
Create comparative safety effect records only when the extraction process can derive or source risk ratios, risk differences, and uncertainty without unsupported assumptions.

provenance.note: Posted aggregate adverse-event counts for D+Q and untreated-control event groups.

provenance.locator: PubMed abstract serious adverse event sentence
provenance.note: Abstract reports no serious adverse events were observed.
Publication table provides descriptive adverse-event term counts and the safety section describes serious/severe-event and withdrawal context.

name: D+Q bone RCT 2-week SASP synthesis compatibility
question: Can the 2-week SASP result from the D+Q postmenopausal bone RCT be pooled for a meta-analysis estimate?

compatibility_basis.population: Postmenopausal women in the D+Q and untreated-control arms of one phase 2 randomized trial.
compatibility_basis.endpoint_family: Plasma SASP marker burden percent change.
compatibility_basis.effect_metric: Marker-defined SASP percent-change effect with uncertainty.
compatibility_basis.variance_model: No pooling model selected because only one broad SASP result is available and marker-level identities plus uncertainty are absent.

pooling_rationale: Pooling is blocked by result count, broad marker definition, and p-value-only extraction. This group has one SASP result from one trial. The registry record provides group medians, full ranges, and a p-value for a broad SASP burden measure, but not marker-level identities, an effect value, or a variance-compatible uncertainty estimate.
non_pooling_reason: Do not pool SASP effects until marker identity is harmonized and at least two compatible marker-level or composite SASP results provide effect value, uncertainty, comparison, sample size, and group-value fields.

missing_effect_fields_by_result.note: The SASP result is a broad p-value-only measure without marker-level identity, effect value, or CI, standard error, or variance.

self_healing_actions: Keep SASP pooling blocked until marker-level identity and uncertainty fields are extracted.
Route SASP extraction to extraction_refresh if publication tables or supplements identify the marker panel and effect uncertainty.

provenance.note: Posted broad SASP outcome group values and D+Q versus untreated-control p-value.
